sections.paragraph:
fut. יִשְׁלַט a word of the later Hebrew-

(1) to rule over any one, followed by בְּ Ecc 2:19 8:9 and עַל Neh 5:15.

(2) to obtain power, get the mastery, followed by בְּ Est 9:1. (Arab. سَلِطَ to be hard, vehement, whence שַׁלִּיט No. 1, שֶׁלֶט; the power of ruling appears only in the derivative nouns, as سُلطْاَنُ power, hence the concr. Sultan. To this appears to answer Germ. fchalten).

Hiphil-

(1) to cause to rule, Psa 119:133.

(2) to give power over any thing, Ecc 5:18 6:2 . Compare מָשַׁל Exo 21:8.

Derivatives, שֶׁלֶט-שָׁלְטָן, שַׁלִּיט.
